chi: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Chinese.
chin: noun. The bottom of a face, the typically jutting jawline below the mouth.
chug: noun. A dull, fairly quick explosive or percussive sound, as if made by a labouring engine.
churn: verb. To agitate rapidly and repetitively, or to stir with a rowing or rocking motion; generally applies to liquids, notably cream.
crush: noun. A violent collision or compression; a crash; destruction; ruin.
crushing: verb. present participle and gerund of crush
cuish: noun. Alternative form of cuisse (“thigh armour”).
cur: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chhulung.
girns: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of girn
gnu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gnau.
grin: noun. A smile in which the lips are parted to reveal the teeth.
gun: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mbya Guarani.
hin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Hindi.
ich: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Etkywan.
inch: noun. An English unit of length equal to 1/12 of a foot or 2.54 cm, conceived as roughly the width of a thumb.
incur: verb. To bring upon oneself or expose oneself to, especially something inconvenient, harmful, or onerous; to become liable or subject to.
incurs: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of incur
incus: noun. A small anvil -shaped bone in the middle ear.
inrush: noun. A crowding or flooding in.
ins: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Indian Sign Language.
nighs: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of nigh
nus: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nuer.
rich: adjective. Wealthy: having a lot of money and possessions.
rig: noun. The rigging of a sailing ship or other such craft.
rin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nungu.
ring: noun. A solid object in the shape of a circle.
rins: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of rin
rug: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Roviana.
ruin: noun. The remains of a destroyed or dilapidated construction, such as a house or castle.
ruing: verb. present participle and gerund of rue
run: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Kirundi.
rung: noun. A crosspiece forming a step of a ladder; a round.
runic: adjective. Of, pertaining to, or written using runes.
rushing: verb. present participle and gerund of rush
shin: noun. The front part of the leg below the knee; the front edge of the shin bone: Shinbone on Wikipedia.
shrug: noun. A lifting of the shoulders to signal indifference or a casual lack of knowledge.
shun: verb. To avoid, especially persistently; ostracize.
sic: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Malinguat.
sigh: verb. To inhale a larger quantity of air than usual, and immediately expel it; to make a deep single audible respiration, especially as the
sign: noun. A visible fact that shows that something exists or may happen.
sir: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Siri.
sri: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Siriano.
ugh: interjection. Used to express repugnance, disgust, or annoyance.
uric: adjective. Pertaining to, contained in, or obtained from urine.
urn: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Uruangnirin.
using: verb. present participle and gerund of use
